mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate directly lower corporate borrowing costs and compress equity discount rates, generating positive forward returns for the S&P 500 over the next month.
failure_condition: Rank IC falls below 0.02 over a 3-year rolling window or forward returns show no statistical significance against zero during rate cuts.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly unemployment rate increase exceeding 0.3 percentage points signals sudden labor market deterioration, triggering immediate risk-off positioning and negative S&P 500 returns within one week.
failure_condition: Positive 5-day forward returns occur in more than 55% of triggered instances or the signal fails to predict direction with p < 0.05.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in real personal consumption expenditures and housing starts indicates broad-based consumer demand strength, driving sustained positive equity returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Joint positive growth in RSAFS and HOUST fails to produce positive 60-day returns in at least 60% of historical observations.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production during periods of low inflation reflects healthy capacity utilization without pricing pressure, supporting positive S&P 500 forward returns over one month.
failure_condition: The signal loses predictive power when CPIAUCSL month-over-month growth exceeds 0.5%, or forward returns turn negative in > 50% of cases.

mechanism_narrative: Higher real GDP growth directly increases corporate earnings expectations, producing positive short-term S&P 500 returns as markets price in improved profitability.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns show negative correlation with GDPC1 growth or Rank IC remains below 0.01 across all tested windows.

mechanism_narrative: A federal funds rate level exceeding 2.0 percent combined with rapid tightening triggers liquidity constraints that suppress equity valuations over the subsequent quarter.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns remain positive in more than 50% of periods where FEDFUNDS exceeds the 2.0% threshold.

mechanism_narrative: Strong nonfarm payroll growth alongside declining unemployment confirms robust labor market tightening, which boosts consumer spending forecasts and drives positive monthly equity returns.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict positive 20-day returns when PAYEMS growth falls below 100k while UNRATE declines.

mechanism_narrative: Declining housing starts during a monetary tightening cycle signal reduced construction activity and lower near-term economic momentum, causing negative short-term S&P 500 returns.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are positive in > 55% of triggered instances or the signal shows no statistical edge during tightening phases.
